title: Stability and dynamics of dark-bright solitons in spin-orbit- and Rabi-coupled binary Bose-Einstein condensates
authors: K. Rajaswathi, R. Ravisankar, R. Radha, P. K. Mishra, P. Muruganandam
abstract: We investigate the stability and nonlinear dynamics of dark-bright solitons in a one-dimensional binary Bose-Einstein condensate subjected to synthetic spin-orbit and Rabi couplings. In the absence of spin-orbit coupling, we map the coupled Gross-Pitaevskii equations onto the integrable Manakov model and obtain exact dark-bright soliton solutions, providing a rigorous theoretical benchmark. We demonstrate that finite spin-orbit coupling breaks integrability by inducing spin-dependent phase gradients, which result in spatial separation of the spin components and the emergence of intrinsic density oscillations. By contrast, Rabi coupling enforces phase locking between components and supports robust breather-like excitations. Using imaginary-time propagation together with Bogoliubov-de Gennes analysis, we systematically characterise ground-state phases and excitation spectra for both symmetric and asymmetric interaction regimes in homogeneous and harmonically trapped systems. Real-time simulations further demonstrate that finite gauge fields and interaction quenches drive the system far from equilibrium, giving rise to diverse nonlinear phenomena, including multi-soliton fragmentation, breathing stripe patterns, and soliton dynamics. Our results highlight the interplay of synthetic gauge fields, external confinement, and interaction engineering as powerful tools for controlling the stability and dynamical behaviour of nonlinear excitations in multicomponent quantum gases.
summary: This paper explores how spin-orbit and Rabi couplings modify the stability and dynamics of dark-bright solitons in two-component Bose-Einstein condensates. It reveals that while these couplings break the integrability of the system, they also enable the creation of complex nonlinear phenomena like breathing stripes and soliton fragmentation. The findings highlight the potential for using synthetic gauge fields to precisely control nonlinear structures in ultracold atoms.
main_problem: Investigating the stability and nonlinear dynamics of dark-bright solitons in a binary Bose-Einstein condensate subjected to synthetic spin-orbit and Rabi couplings.
main_result: The study demonstrates that spin-orbit coupling breaks integrability and induces spatial separation and density oscillations, while Rabi coupling promotes phase locking and breather-like excitations; interaction quenches further drive the system into complex regimes like multi-soliton fragmentation and stripe patterns.
method: Numerical simulations using imaginary-time propagation for ground states, real-time evolution via Split-Step Crank-Nicolson, and Bogoliubov-de Gennes analysis for stability and excitation spectra.
model_or_system: A one-dimensional, pseudo-spin-1/2 binary Bose-Einstein condensate described by coupled Gross-Pitaevskii equations featuring spin-orbit coupling (kL), Rabi coupling (Omega), and inter/intraspecies interactions.
key_observables: Component densities, chemical potentials, energy functionals, population imbalance, and Bogoliubov-de Gennes excitation spectra (eigenfrequencies).
important_parameters: Spin-orbit coupling strength (kL), Rabi coupling strength (Omega), interaction strengths (g_ij), and external harmonic confinement vs. homogeneous (trapless) regimes.
main_assumptions: The system is treated in a quasi-one-dimensional regime with strong transverse confinement; the study focuses on the nonlinear dynamics of the Gross-Pitaevskii framework.
figures_summary: Figures show ground-state density transitions (miscible to immiscible), soliton profiles in harmonic traps, spatiotemporal evolution of densities under increasing coupling strengths, and complex patterns (fragmentation, stripes) following interaction quenches in both trapped and free space.
paper_structure: The paper establishes a theoretical benchmark using the integrable Manakov model, analyzes the impact of spin-orbit and Rabi couplings on soliton stability via BdG analysis, and explores non-equilibrium dynamics through real-time simulations of interaction and coupling quenches.
why_interesting: It provides deep insights into how synthetic gauge fields and coherent coupling can be used to engineer and control nonlinear excitations and complex pattern formation in multicomponent quantum gases.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Spatiotemporal evolution of component densities |ψ↑|2 and |ψ↓|2 for a dark-bright soliton with kL = 0. The top row (a1-d1) shows analytical density profiles based on the Manakov solution, while the bottom row (a2-d2) presents the corresponding numerical results obtained via the SSCN method. The first two columns (a1, b1 and a2, b2) correspond to the integrable regime at Ω= 0, showing shape-preserving propagation. The last two columns (c1, d1 and c2, d2) illus- trate the dynamics at Ω= 2.0, where Rabi coupling induces population transfer between components. The numerical re- sults follow the analytical benchmarks in both regimes. The soliton parameters in Eqs. (7a) and (7b) are fixed...

FIG. 2. Ground-state density profiles of the two components, |ψ↑|2 (solid red lines) and |ψ↓|2 (dashed blue lines), along with the total density (black dotted lines), obtained via imaginary-time evolution using Gaussian initial wave functions. No spin-orbit or Rabi coupling is present (kL = 0, Ω= 0). The system is confined by an external trap, with intraspecies interaction strengths fixed at g↑↑= g↓↓= 2. Panels (a1)-(a3) show the ground-state densities for interspecies interaction strengths g↑↓= g↓↑= 1, 2.7, and 4, respectively. The corresponding Bogoliubov-de Gennes excitation spectra are shown in panels (b1)-(b3). The chemical potentials for the ground states in panels (a1)-(a3) are µ↑=...

FIG. 3. Ground-state (non-trivial) density profiles of a two-component Bose-Einstein condensate, |ψ↑|2 and |ψ↓|2, obtained via imaginary-time evolution using Gaussian initial wave functions superimposed with dark-bright soliton profiles from equation (5). No spin-orbit or Rabi coupling is present (kL = 0, Ω= 0), and the system is confined by an external trapping potential. The intraspecies interaction strengths are fixed at g↑↑= g↓↓= 2. Panels (a1) and (a2) show the ground-state densities for interspecies interaction strengths g↑↓= g↓↑= 2.7 and 4.0, respectively. The corresponding Bogoliubov-de Gennes excitation spectra are shown in panels (b1) and (b2), exhibiting real eigenfrequencies....

FIG. 4. Time evolution of dark-bright soliton densities inside a harmonic trap under varying spin-orbit coupling strength. Panels (a) and (b) show |ψ↑|2 and |ψ↓|2, respectively, for kL increasing from 0 to 1 with Ω= 0. Panels (c) and (d) show |ψ↑|2 and |ψ↓|2, respectively, for kL increasing from 0 to 2 with Ω= 0. The nonlinearity parameters are fixed at g↑↑= g↓↓= 2.0 and g↑↓= g↓↑= 2.7.
